segments.text: After these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill him.
Now the Jews' feast of tabernacles was at hand.
Depart hence, and go into Judæa, that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest.
segments.speaker: Jesus' Brethren

segments.text: For there is no man that doeth any thing in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly. If thou do these things, shew thyself to the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus' Brethren

segments.text: For neither did his brethren believe in him.
Then Jesus said unto them,
When he had said these words unto them, he abode still in Galilee.
But when his brethren were gone up, then went he also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.
Where is he?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: And there was much murmuring among the people concerning him: for some said, He is a good man: others said, Nay; but he deceiveth the people.
Howbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.
Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.
How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: Jesus answered them, and said,
Thou hast a devil: who goeth about to kill thee?
segments.speaker: The People

segments.text: Jesus answered and said unto them,
Is not this he, whom they seek to kill?
segments.speaker: Some from Jerusalem

segments.text: But, lo, he speaketh boldly, and they say nothing unto him. Do the rulers know indeed that this is the very Christ?
segments.speaker: Some from Jerusalem

segments.text: Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught, saying,
When Christ cometh, will he do more miracles than these which this man hath done?
segments.speaker: Many of the People

segments.text: The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.
Then said Jesus unto them,
Whither will he go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersed among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: What manner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying,
(But this spake he of the Spirit, which they that believe on him should receive: for the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.)
Of a truth this is the Prophet.
segments.speaker: Many of the People

segments.text: This is the Christ.
segments.speaker: Some People

segments.text: Shall Christ come out of Galilee?
segments.speaker: Other People

segments.text: Why have ye not brought him?
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Pharisees

segments.text: Never man spake like this man.
segments.speaker: The Officers

segments.text: Are ye also deceived?
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: Nicodemus saith unto them, (he that came to Jesus by night, being one of them,)
Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?
segments.speaker: Nicodemus

segments.text: Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: And every man went unto his own house.
